Business question: Show all game names played by at least 1000 hours.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Plays_games (gameid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Video_games (gameid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_plays_games.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT gameid AS game_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Plays_games') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_video_games.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT gameid AS game_id, gname AS game_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'Video_games') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Plays_games
      - name: Video_games
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_plays_games
    columns:
      - name: game_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_video_games
    columns:
      - name: game_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: game_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_popular_games.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH game_plays AS (
  SELECT
    p.game_id,
    v.game_name,
    hours_played
  FROM {{ ref('stg_plays_games') }} p
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_video_games') }} v ON p.game_id = v.game_id
)
SELECT
  game_name
FROM game_plays
GROUP BY game_id, game_name
HAVING SUM(hours_played) >= 1000
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_popular_games
    description: "List of games played for at least 1000 hours."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per game"
    columns:
      - name: game_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is the last name of the musician that has been at the back position the most?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Band (lastname VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Performance (bandmate VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_band.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    lastname,
    id AS band_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Band') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_performance.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    bandmate,
    stageposition
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Performance') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Band
      - name: Performance
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_band
    description: "Staging table for band members."
    columns:
      - name: lastname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: band_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
  - name: stg_performance
    description: "Staging table for performance data."
    columns:
      - name: bandmate
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stageposition
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_back_position_musicians.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH back_performances AS (
    SELECT
        b.lastname,
        COUNT(*) AS performance_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_performance') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_band') }} b ON p.bandmate = b.band_id
    WHERE p.stageposition = 'back'
    GROUP BY b.lastname
)
SELECT
    lastname AS back_position_musician_last_name,
    performance_count
FROM back_performances
ORDER BY performance_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_back_position_musicians
    description: "Musician with the most performances at back position."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per musician with most back performances"
    columns:
      - name: back_position_musician_last_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: performance_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the maximum training hours for the students whose training hours is greater than 1000 in different positions?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE player (HS INTEGER, pID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE tryout (pPos VARCHAR, pID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_player.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT HS AS training_hours, pID AS player_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'player') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_tryout.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT pPos AS position, pID AS player_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'tryout') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: player
      - name: tryout
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_player
    columns:
      - name: training_hours
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_tryout
    columns:
      - name: position
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_max_training_hours.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    p.training_hours,
    t.position
  FROM {{ ref('stg_player') }} AS p
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_tryout') }} AS t ON p.player_id = t.player_id
  WHERE p.training_hours > 1000
)
SELECT
  position,
  MAX(training_hours) AS max_training_hours
FROM base
GROUP BY position
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_max_training_hours
    description: "Maximum training hours by position for students with >1000 hours."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per position"
    columns:
      - name: position
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: max_training_hours
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of airports which do not have any flight in and out.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Flights (AirportName VARCHAR, AirportCode VARCHAR, SourceAirport VARCHAR, DestAirport VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Airports (AirportName VARCHAR, AirportCode VARCHAR, SourceAirport VARCHAR, DestAirport VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    AirportName AS airport_name,
    AirportCode AS airport_code,
    SourceAirport AS source_airport,
    DestAirport AS dest_airport
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Flights') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    AirportName AS airport_name,
    AirportCode AS airport_code,
    SourceAirport AS source_airport,
    DestAirport AS dest_airport
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Airports') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Flights
      - name: Airports
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_flights
    description: "Cleaned flights data."
    columns:
      - name: airport_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airport_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: source_airport
      - name: dest_airport
  - name: stg_airports
    description: "Cleaned airports data."
    columns:
      - name: airport_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airport_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_airports_without_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH airports AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }}
),
flights_involved AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT SourceAirport FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT DestAirport FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
)
SELECT
    a.airport_name,
    a.airport_code
FROM airports a
WHERE NOT a.airport_code IN (
    SELECT SourceAirport FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
    UNION
    SELECT DestAirport FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_airports_without_flights
    description: "List of airports with no incoming or outgoing flights."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per airport without flights"
    columns:
      - name: airport_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airport_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Show the name of the shop that has the most kind of devices in stock.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE shop (Shop_Name VARCHAR, Shop_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE stock (Shop_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_shop.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT shop_id, shop_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'shop') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_stock.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT shop_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'stock') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: shop
      - name: stock
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_shop
    columns:
      - name: shop_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_stock
    columns:
      - name: shop_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_shop_with_most_devices.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH shop_stock AS (
  SELECT
    s.shop_id,
    s.shop_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_stock') }} st
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_shop') }} s ON st.shop_id = s.shop_id
),
device_counts AS (
  SELECT
    shop_id,
    COUNT(*) AS device_count
  FROM shop_stock
  GROUP BY shop_id
)
SELECT
  s.shop_name AS shop_with_most_devices
FROM device_counts dc
JOIN shop_stock s ON dc.shop_id = s.shop_id
ORDER BY device_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_shop_with_most_devices
    description: "Shop that has the most kind of devices in stock."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per shop with most devices"
    columns:
      - name: shop_with_most_devices
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: On what date was Tie #13 played?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE table_name_93 (date VARCHAR, tie_no VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_table_name_93.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    date AS tie_date,
    tie_no AS tie_number
FROM {{ source('raw', 'table_name_93') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: table_name_93
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_table_name_93
    description: "Staging table for tie information with date and number."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/dim_tie_dates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        tie_date,
        tie_number
    FROM {{ ref('stg_table_name_93') }}
)

    tie_date AS date_tie_13_played
FROM base
WHERE tie_number = '13'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

    description: "Dimension table containing the date when Tie #13 was played."
    meta:
      model_type: dimension
      grain: "1 row per tie number"
</file>

Business question: Show institution names along with the number of proteins for each institution.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE institution (institution VARCHAR, institution_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE protein (institution_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_institution.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    institution_id,
    institution AS institution_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'institution') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_protein.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    institution_id,
    protein_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'protein') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: institution
      - name: protein
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_institution
    columns:
      - name: institution_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_protein
    columns:
      - name: protein_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_protein_count_by_institution.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        i.institution_id,
        i.institution_name,
        p.protein_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_institution') }} AS i
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_protein') }} AS p
        ON i.institution_id = p.institution_id
)
SELECT
    institution_name,
    COUNT(*) AS protein_count
FROM base
GROUP BY institution_id, institution_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_protein_count_by_institution
    description: "Number of proteins per institution."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per institution"
    columns:
      - name: institution_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: protein_count
        tests: [not_null, positive]
</file>

Business question: Find the names of nurses who are on call.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE on_call (nurse VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE nurse (name VARCHAR, EmployeeID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_nurse.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS nurse_name,
    EmployeeID AS employee_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'nurse') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_on_call.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    nurse AS on_call_nurse
FROM {{ source('raw', 'on_call') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: nurse
      - name: on_call
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_nurse
    columns:
      - name: nurse_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_on_call
    columns:
      - name: on_call_nurse
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_nurses_on_call.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        n.nurse_name,
        o.on_call_nurse
    FROM {{ ref('stg_nurse') }} AS n
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_on_call') }} AS o ON n.employee_id = o.on_call_nurse
)
SELECT DISTINCT nurse_name AS nurse_name_on_call
FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_nurses_on_call
    description: "List of nurses who are currently on call."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per nurse on call"
    columns:
      - name: nurse_name_on_call
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_course_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    date_of_enrolment AS date_of_enrolment,

    student_id AS student_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    student_id AS student_id,
    family_name AS family_name,

FROM {{ source('raw', 'Students') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:

      - name: Student_Course_Enrolment

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_zieme_bernie_dates.sql">

WITH base AS (

        e.date_of_enrolment,

    date_of_completion

Business question: What is the last name of the staff who has handled the first ever complaint?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE staff (last_name VARCHAR, staff_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE complaints (staff_id VARCHAR, date_complaint_raised VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    last_name,
    staff_id AS staff_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_complaints.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_id,
    date_complaint_raised AS date_complaint_raised
FROM {{ source('raw', 'complaints') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: staff
      - name: complaints
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_staff
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_complaints
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_first_complaint_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        s.last_name,
        c.date_complaint_raised
    FROM {{ ref('stg_staff') }} s
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_complaints') }} c ON s.staff_id = c.staff_id
),
ordered AS (
    SELECT
        last_name,
        date_complaint_raised,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY date_complaint_raised) AS row_num
    FROM base
)
SELECT
    last_name AS staff_last_name
FROM ordered
WHERE row_num = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_first_complaint_staff
    description: "Last name of the staff who handled the first ever complaint."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per first complaint"
    columns:
      - name: staff_last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

CREATE TABLE MEMBER_OF (DNO VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE DEPARTMENT (DName VARCHAR, DNO VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_member_of.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT DNO FROM {{ source('raw', 'MEMBER_OF') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT DName, DNO FROM {{ source('raw', 'DEPARTMENT') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: MEMBER_OF
      - name: DEPARTMENT
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_member_of
    columns:
      - name: DNO
        tests: [not_null]

    columns:
      - name: DName
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: DNO
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_smallest_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT d.DName, m.DNO

  JOIN {{ ref('stg_member_of') }} AS m ON d.DNO = m.DNO
)
SELECT DName, COUNT(*) AS member_count
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY DNO, DName
ORDER BY member_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

</file>

Business question: Find the number of different products that are produced by companies at different headquarter cities.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE manufacturers (Headquarter VARCHAR, code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE products (name VARCHAR, Manufacturer VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: manufacturers
      - name: products
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_manufacturers

      - name: headquarter
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: name

      - name: manufacturer

<file path="models/staging/stg_manufacturers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Headquarter AS headquarter,
    code AS code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'manufacturers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS name,
    Manufacturer AS manufacturer
FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_products_by_headquarter.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.name,
        m.headquarter
    FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }} AS p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_manufacturers') }} AS m ON p.manufacturer = m.code

FROM base
GROUP BY headquarter
</file>

    columns:
      - name: headquarter
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: distinct_product_count
        tests: [not_null, positive]
</file>
